matcher.subject: Mathematics
matcher.coveragePreview: surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes grade 9 mathematics students cube and cuboid geometry cylinder, cone, and sphere formulas hemisphere properties application of surface area concepts surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes introduction to surface area and volume mathematics: surface areas & volumes focus: cubes, cylinders, cones, spheres what we will learn today learning objectives for surface areas and volumes identify properties of cubes, cuboids, cylinders, cones, spheres, and hemispheres. recall and apply formulas for total surface area (tsa) and curved surface area (csa). calculate volume for all standard 3d shapes. solve real-world problems involving surface area and volume. defining surface area and volume definitions of surface area and volume surface area: total area covered by all faces/surfaces. measured in square units (m², cm²). volume: space enclosed within the boundaries. measured in cubic units (m³, cm³). key distinction: 2d vs. 3d measurement. cubes and cuboids: formulas & logic cube and cuboid surface area/volume formulas cube (side = a): - total surface area: 6a² - curved/total sa is same. lateral sa: 4a² - volume: a³ cuboid (length=l, breadth=b, height=h):...
